description: NOTE: commercial fields on this entity (price, paymentTerms, dieCylinderCharges, documentationCharges) are hiddenForRoles ["quality"] - stripped server-side and client-side per axiom A17. A supplier's structured response to an RFQ (SCOR S1.8/S1.9). The supplier is the sole editor of its own quote via a unique authenticated link; negotiation re-opens the SAME form under the SAME quotationId, versioned with revisedAt, so multiple rounds are tracked without re-keying. Carries the headline price, payment/delivery terms, incoterm (validated against transport mode), and the supplier's separable charges (die/cylinder, documentation). Per-line detail lives on QuotationLine. The system computes normalized landed cost on top of the quote for ranking; the quote itself is what the supplier owns.

fields.name: quotationId
fields.label: Quotation No.
fields.validation: system-generated; unique; immutable; the SAME id persists across negotiation revisions (revisedAt versions it, the id does not change)
fields.purpose: Stable key for one supplier's quote so the comparison, negotiation versions, and the eventual award all attach to one record; immutability across revisions is what keeps the negotiation history coherent.

fields.name: rfqId
fields.label: RFQ
fields.validation: must reference a SENT RFQ; immutable; supplier must be in the RFQ's invitedSupplierIds
fields.purpose: Links the quote to its sourcing event so all responses to one RFQ compare line-for-line and trace back to the requisition.

fields.name: supplierId
fields.label: Supplier
fields.validation: the authenticated supplier on the unique link; must be in invitedSupplierIds; read-only; one quotation per (rfqId, supplierId)
fields.purpose: Identifies whose quote this is, scoped by the email+OTP unique link so a supplier can only see and edit its own quote; the sole-editor rule is enforced here.

fields.name: price
fields.label: Quoted Price
fields.validation: > 0; quote currency; for item RFQs this is the headline total, per-line unit prices on QuotationLine
fields.purpose: The headline quoted amount that, after landed-cost normalization, drives ranking and the abnormal-price-spike flag (>105% of last purchase price). The number the comparison is built on.

fields.name: currency
fields.label: Quote Currency
fields.validation: a configured currency master; landed cost is FX-normalized to base for cross-currency comparison (axiom A12)
fields.purpose: The currency the quote is priced in; the FX service normalizes the landed cost to base so quotes in different currencies rank on one comparable figure.

fields.name: paymentTerms
fields.label: Payment Terms
fields.validation: one of the PaymentTerms enum (100% advance | part-advance + against documents | part-advance + against shipment | 30/70 | net 30 | net 60 | net 90)
fields.purpose: The supplier's offered payment terms; a commercial dimension of the comparison and the terms that flow to the PO and payment schedule if this quote is awarded.

fields.name: deliveryTerms
fields.label: Delivery Terms
fields.validation: non-empty; the supplier's delivery basis (place, timing)
fields.purpose: States how and when the supplier will deliver; informs the delivery-time input to ranking and the delivery terms captured in the contract/PO.

fields.name: incoterm
fields.label: Incoterm
fields.validation: one of Incoterms 2020; VALIDATED against transportMode at every submit/revise: FOB/CIF/CFR are sea/inland-waterway only, air/road/courier/multimodal use FCA/CPT/CIP/DAP, EXW is mode-agnostic; invalid combination rejects the submission
fields.purpose: Allocates cost and risk between supplier and buyer (the basis for which landed-cost components the buyer bears) and which incoterm flows to the PO; the transport-mode validation prevents physically impossible terms (sea-only incoterms on an air shipment).

fields.name: transportMode
fields.label: Transport Mode
fields.mandatoryCondition: required for item/import quotes; not used for service quotes
fields.validation: one of Air | Sea | Road | Courier; the value the incoterm is validated against
fields.purpose: The shipping mode; pairs with incoterm so the system can reject sea-only incoterms (FOB/CIF/CFR) on non-sea shipments and carries into inbound-logistics tracking after the PO.

fields.name: dieCylinderCharges
fields.label: Die / Cylinder Charges
fields.validation: >= 0; quote currency; a separable tooling charge (printing dies/cylinders), one-time
fields.purpose: Captures one-time tooling cost separately from unit price so the buyer sees the true first-order cost and can amortize or negotiate the tooling distinctly; folds into landed cost via miscellaneous.

fields.name: documentationCharges
fields.label: Documentation Charges
fields.validation: >= 0; quote currency; charge for export/shipping documents
fields.purpose: Captures the supplier's documentation fee separately so it is visible in the comparison and rolls into the landed-cost documentation component rather than hiding inside unit price.

fields.name: notes
fields.label: Supplier Notes
fields.purpose: Free-text the supplier adds (caveats, validity, MOQ, lead-time notes) that the buyer reads alongside the price during comparison and negotiation.

fields.name: attachments
fields.label: Attachments
fields.validation: optional files (quote PDF, spec sheets, COA/MSDS for regulated items)
fields.purpose: Supporting documents the supplier uploads; for regulated items the COA/MSDS attached here feeds the Quality hard gate before selection.

fields.name: submittedAt
fields.label: Submitted At
fields.validation: now() on first submit; compared to RFQ.deadline to flag late responses
fields.purpose: Timestamps the original submission; vs the RFQ deadline it marks on-time/late and feeds the supplier responsiveness dimension of the scorecard.

fields.name: revisedAt
fields.label: Last Revised At
fields.validation: now() each time the supplier re-opens the same form and resubmits a negotiated price; null until first revision
fields.purpose: Versions the quote across negotiation rounds under the same quotationId so the negotiation history and the latest agreed price are both auditable; each revision re-runs the incoterm validation.

fields.name: status
fields.label: Status
fields.validation: SUBMITTED -> UNDER_NEGOTIATION -> REVISED (loops) -> SELECTED | REJECTED; SELECTED requires Quality COA/MSDS approval for regulated items
fields.purpose: Where the quote stands in the sourcing event; drives the available actions (negotiate, select, reject) and records which quote was chosen vs disqualified (e.g. a regulated item rejected at the QC gate).

invariants: A1 the requisition identifier (via the RFQ) is immutable end to end
A6 award approval reuses the approval engine with SoD (award approver != selecting buyer)
A12 cross-currency quotes are normalized to base for ranking
A13 each submission and revision is audited and broadcast
A16 lines on this quotation are awarded independently; awarding lines from multiple suppliers emits one PurchaseOrder per distinct winning supplier
the supplier is the sole editor of its own quote (sourcing invariant)
negotiation re-opens the same form under the same quotationId, versioned by revisedAt
incoterm validated against transportMode (FOB/CIF/CFR sea-only) at every submit/revise
a regulated item cannot be SELECTED without Quality COA/MSDS approval (hard gate)
